data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260508T040408-f6f0cd19/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260508T040408-f6f0cd19/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260508T040408-f6f0cd19/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260508T040408-f6f0cd19/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260508T040408-f6f0cd19/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 2. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-1c0de0af">
    <title>agent: replace user-facing complexity and intake terminology</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
Operators see confusing `ddx work` messages such as `pre-claim intake: pre-claim intake: empty output (continuing to claim ddx-cce893be)` and `pre-dispatch lint: lint hook: missing-harness (continuing to claim ddx-cce893be)`. The wording exposes internal hook names, repeats prefixes, and fails to distinguish bead-readiness warnings from system-readiness warnings such as missing harness or empty hook output.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go:86-107` defines `ComplexityGuard` and prints `complexity gate missing; allowing bead`.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:670-710` prints `pre-claim intake` messages directly to the operator log, including fail-open `(continuing to claim ...)` text.
- `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:192-205` returns errors already prefixed with `pre-claim intake: ...`; the execute-loop log then prepends another `pre-claim intake:` prefix.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:732-751` prints `pre-dispatch lint` messages directly to the operator log.
- `cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go:22-28` and `cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go:95-97` classify missing harness as `lint hook: missing-harness`, but the user-facing loop message does not explain that readiness checks were unavailable due to agent/harness configuration.
- `cli/internal/config/types.go:307` describes triage config as the pre-Claim complexity gate, and `cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json:651` carries generated stale wording.

PROPOSED FIX
Replace operator-facing terminology with bead readiness language while preserving compatibility for persisted events, prompt sources, and internal structs where unsafe to rename immediately:
- Render hook infrastructure failures as actionable readiness-check warnings, for example `readiness check unavailable: no harness configured; continuing with ddx-cce893be`.
- Render empty hook output as a system/check failure, not as a bead defect, for example `readiness check unavailable: intake returned empty output; continuing with ddx-cce893be`.
- Avoid doubled prefixes by formatting hook errors once at the loop boundary.
- Keep fail-open semantics intact for infrastructure failures and keep blocking semantics intact for real bead-readiness failures.
- Update config comments/schema/docs so user-facing text no longer refers to complexity gates, pre-claim intake, or pre-dispatch lint except where explicitly marked legacy/debug.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not perform large internal type/function renames or event migrations.
- Do not change terminal result-line status markers such as `✓ ddx-id → error`; that is owned by `ddx-f326f001`.
- Do not implement the full readiness/system classification taxonomy; that is owned by `ddx-e2479b81`.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. CLI/operator log output no longer says `complexity gate missing`.
2. CLI/operator log output avoids `pre-claim intake` and `pre-dispatch lint` unless explicitly marked legacy/debug.
3. `TestReadinessUnavailableOutputIsActionable` asserts missing harness renders as an actionable readiness-check/system warning rather than `lint hook: missing-harness`.
4. `TestReadinessHookEmptyOutput_DeduplicatesPrefix` asserts empty intake output is rendered with a single user-facing prefix and does not contain `pre-claim intake: pre-claim intake:`.
5. `TestZeroConfigWork_NoConfigDoesNotEmitUnderSpecified` asserts zero-config work output remains actionable and does not expose stale complexity/intake/lint terminology.
6. Config comments/schema descriptions no longer describe `agent.triage` as a complexity or pre-claim gate.
7. Generated docs and website command/config docs are refreshed if affected.
8. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/config/... -run "Test(Readiness|Intake|Lint|ZeroConfig|Config)" -count=1` passes.
9. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:agent, area:cli, area:config, kind:cleanup, reliability, bead-quality, adr:023, spec:FEAT-010</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-a9d130d0" spec-id="ADR-023" base-rev="28e1a975fa91bb0ec453b2166876ca26dfcda74c" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260508T040408-f6f0cd19"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="ADR-023" path="docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md">ADR-023: Bead Lifecycle Quality Policy</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All pre-commit checks pass and the commit is done. Here's a summary of what was completed:

**Changes made across 5 files:**

1. **`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`** (captured in checkpoint): Added `errors` import; replaced all operator-facing "pre-claim intake" log messages with "readiness check" language; replaced "pre-dispatch lint: %v" with smart detection — `LintHookErrorKindMissingHarness` renders as "readiness check unavailable: no harness configured; continuing with X", other infra errors as "readiness check unavailable: %v"; changed block message from "pre-dispatch lint blocked" to "bead-quality check blocked".

2. **`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_test.go`** (captured in checkpoint): Updated two existing test assertions from "pre-claim intake: starting" / "pre-claim intake warning:" to "readiness check: starting" / "readiness check unavailable:"; added `TestReadinessHookEmptyOutput_DeduplicatesPrefix` and `TestReadinessUnavailableOutputIsActionable`.

3. **`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go`** (captured in checkpoint): Added `TestZeroConfigWork_NoConfigDoesNotEmitUnderSpecified`.

4. **`cli/internal/config/types.go`**: Updated two comments replacing "pre-claim complexity gate" / "pre-Claim complexity gate" with "bead readiness decomposition gate".

5. **`cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json`**: Updated description replacing "pre-claim complexity gate" with "bead readiness decomposition gate".
